title: Cancun 2-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Cancun 2-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Cancun, Mexico: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Chichen Itza day trip and Xcaret eco-archaeological park. Prices from $573 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Cancun, Mexico — Tropical climate with a hot, humid wet season carrying hurricane risk (Jun-Nov) and a warm, drier season (Dec-Apr). December to April for dry, sunny weather with lower humidity and reduced hurricane risk. This itinerary blends Chichen Itza day trip, Xcaret eco-archaeological park, Playa Delfines with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Chichen Itza day trip, Xcaret eco-archaeological park, Playa Delfines
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Cancun
itinerary.description: Land at Cancún International Airport (CUN) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Chichen Itza day trip — UNESCO Maya pyramid complex, guided tour. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Cancun Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Xcaret eco-archaeological park — Cultural and nature park with underground rivers. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to CUN for your onward flight.

travelTips: Agree on taxi fares before getting in, as they don't use meters
Book Chichen Itza tours early morning to beat both heat and tour-bus crowds
Only use reef-safe (mineral, oxybenzone-free) sunscreen at cenotes and eco-parks, as it's often required
Stick to bottled water outside all-inclusive resorts
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Reef-safe sunscreen, mandatory at many eco-parks and cenotes
Swimwear and water shoes for cenotes and reefs
Insect repellent for jungle day trips
thingsToAvoid: Swimming during red or black flag warnings due to strong currents and occasional sargassum seaweed
Timeshare pitch invitations disguised as "free tours" or discounted excursions
Overpaying unmetered taxis without agreeing a fare first
Traveling during peak hurricane season (Aug-Oct) without flexible plans
topRestaurants: Puerto Madero — Argentine steak and seafood ($$$$)
shoppingAreas: Coco Bongo shopping strip Hotel Zone
hiddenGems: El Rey ruins within the Hotel Zone
luxuryExperiences: All-inclusive suite at a five-star Hotel Zone resort
Private yacht charter to Isla Mujeres
Private guided Chichen Itza tour with a gourmet lunch
honeymoonExperiences: Swim-up suite stay at a luxury resort
budgetExperiences: Free public beach at Playa Delfines
Mercado 28 for local food and crafts
Self-guided visit to the El Rey ruins
ADO bus day trip to Playa del Carmen
localEtiquette: Tipping 10-15% is expected at restaurants and for hotel staff.
Greet with a handshake; a warm, personable tone is valued.
Dress modestly when leaving the resort zone into local towns.
Haggling is common at markets but not appropriate in fixed-price stores.
Expect a slower pace outside the tourist zones — plan accordingly.
localTransportGuide: The R-1/R-2 public buses run cheaply and frequently along the Hotel Zone strip to downtown Cancun; taxis should be agreed on a fare before departure since they're unmetered. ADO long-distance buses are a comfortable, affordable way to reach Tulum, Playa del Carmen, or Chichen Itza.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April for dry, sunny weather with lower humidity and reduced hurricane risk.
weather: Tropical climate with a hot, humid wet season carrying hurricane risk (Jun-Nov) and a warm, drier season (Dec-Apr).
visaInformation: Mexico allows visa-free tourist entry for many nationalities (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) for stays up to 180 days; a tourist card (FMM) is issued on arrival or included with the airfare for most visitors flying in.
averageFlightTime: ~15.9h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Cancun?
faqs.answer: Mexico allows visa-free tourist entry for many nationalities (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) for stays up to 180 days; a tourist card (FMM) is issued on arrival or included with the airfare for most visitors flying in.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Cancun?
faqs.answer: December to April for dry, sunny weather with lower humidity and reduced hurricane risk. Tropical climate with a hot, humid wet season carrying hurricane risk (Jun-Nov) and a warm, drier season (Dec-Apr).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Cancun safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Cancun is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Cancun?
faqs.answer: The local currency is MXN. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Cancun is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Private beach dinner at sunset experience. It also works well for families — highlights include Xcaret eco-park.

reviews.author: Yuki Silva
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: El Rey Maya ruins alone was worth the trip, but combined with Xplor adventure park this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Mohammed Ibrahim
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Mercado 28 alone was worth the trip, but combined with Xel-Há all-inclusive water park this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Sarah Santos
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Cancun is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Mercado 28. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Ethan Wang
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Xcaret eco-archaeological park and Tulum ruins day trip. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Chen Smith
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Playa Las Perlas in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Omar Silva
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Coco Bongo show and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Mohammed Khan
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Isla Mujeres ferry & snorkel tour alone was worth the trip, but combined with Coco Bongo show this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Grace Garcia
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Playa Delfines and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Daniel Costa
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Xcaret eco-archaeological park and Isla Mujeres downtown streets. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Olivia Nguyen
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Our trip to Cancun was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Underwater Museum of Art (MUSA) tour was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Grace Kowalski
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Isla Mujeres downtown streets and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Anna van Dijk
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Cancun is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Malecón Tajamar boardwalk. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Liam Ivanov
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Our trip to Cancun was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Playa Las Perlas was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
